---
# =====================================================================
#  Press release — EPA compliance workshops paper
#  Conventions: style-guides/press-release/README.md
#  Template:    formats/press-release/press-release-template.qmd
#  Render:  quarto render epa-compliance-workshops_press-release.qmd
#  Design system: ../../../formats/research-highlight/_style.tex
# =====================================================================
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry:
      - top=0.6in
      - bottom=0.55in
      - left=0.75in
      - right=0.75in
    include-in-header:
      - file: ../../../formats/research-highlight/_style.tex
    keep-tex: true
---

```{=latex}
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MASTHEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\briefheader{\includegraphics[height=0.45in]{../../../formats/logos/eil-logo-maroon.png}}{Press Release}

% Body type for this release (overrides the _style.tex 9.4pt default)
\fontsize{11pt}{15pt}\selectfont

% Gap below the masthead rule to the headline (~7pt, matching the research
% highlight's headsep; the briefheader macro adds -3pt, so 10pt nets ~7pt)
\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% HEADLINE + SUBHEAD
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
{\hyphenpenalty=10000\exhyphenpenalty=10000
\brieftitle{Free EPA Compliance Workshops Did Not Reduce Pollution Violations, Study Finds}}
\vspace{1pt}
\noindent{\color{cite}\rmfamily\itshape\fontsize{11pt}{14pt}\selectfont A program the EPA is now expanding to more states showed no measurable effect on the facilities it was meant to help.\par}
\vspace{2pt}
\noindent\textcolor{warmrule}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.7pt}}

\vspace{8pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% RELEASE LINE + DATELINE (opens the body, AEI-style) + LEAD
% Embargo form: replace with EMBARGOED UNTIL [Month Day, Year, Time TZ]
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\bfseries\color{accentred}\fontsize{8.25pt}{10pt}\selectfont\textls[50]{FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE}}\hspace{0.6em}{\color{muted}\fontsize{9pt}{12pt}\selectfont [CITY, State] · [Month Day, Year]}\par
\vspace{5pt}
\noindent Offering polluters free help to follow environmental rules did not make them any more likely to follow them, according to a new study of hundreds of wastewater treatment facilities in Ohio. The research, published in the \textit{Journal of Policy Analysis and Management}, found that compliance workshops promoted by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) had no detectable effect on violations.

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BODY
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent The study, ``The effect of compliance assistance on pollution discharges and violations of environmental regulations,'' by Paul J. Ferraro and Jay P. Shimshack, examined a program built on a simple premise: that many facilities break environmental rules out of confusion or limited expertise rather than deliberate corner-cutting. Over the past decade, this ``compliance assistance'' approach --- free workshops, guidance, and technical support --- has become the EPA's preferred alternative to inspections and fines.

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent To test whether the help actually changed behavior, the researchers used detailed Ohio EPA records of violations and pollution discharges at facilities that had recently been cited and had volunteered to attend. Since the workshops reached different facilities at different times, the researchers could compare facilities trained earlier with comparable facilities that had also volunteered but were not yet trained. Measured against that benchmark, violations at trained facilities did not fall.

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent The result is notable because the program had appeared to work: the EPA's own before-and-after figures showed violations dropping by more than 50 percent after the workshops. But the same decline was occurring at facilities that had not been trained at all --- a broad downward trend that was easy to mistake for the effect of the workshops themselves.

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent Encouraged by that apparent success, the EPA has moved to expand the program to other states --- committing public funding to an approach with no measurable effect on pollution. This is not to say that assistance can never help; it's one program, and small benefits cannot be ruled out. The broader lesson, they note, is that such programs should be rigorously tested before they replace enforcement tools already known to work.

\vspace{10pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% END MARKER
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{center}{\color{faint}\bfseries \#\#\#}\end{center}

\vspace{6pt}
\noindent\textcolor{warmrule}{\rule{\linewidth}{0.7pt}}
\vspace{4pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% MEDIA CONTACT  (bottom, before boilerplate)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\color{muted}\fontsize{8.25pt}{11pt}\selectfont
For questions, interviews, or other media inquiries, contact [Full Name] at [email] or [phone].\par}

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% BOILERPLATE  (reusable one sentence; supply approved text)
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\color{muted}\fontsize{8.25pt}{11.5pt}\selectfont
{\bfseries About the Environmental Inequality Lab (EIL).} The Environmental Inequality Lab is a nonpartisan research group that applies a rigorous, data-driven approach to understand how our environment shapes economic opportunity and well-being.\par}

\vspace{6pt}

% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
% PAPER LINK
% ---------------------------------------------------------------------
\noindent{\color{faint}\fontsize{7.4pt}{10pt}\selectfont
Full paper: \href{https://doi.org/10.1002/pam.70056}{\color{faint}\underline{doi.org/10.1002/pam.70056}} \quad·\quad
Research highlight: \href{[URL]}{\color{faint}\underline{[link]}}\par}
```
